rationale: Economics at L3 follows the AEA Journal of Economic Literature (JEL) sub-classifications. Thirty-three leaves split by field. Labor economics gets 4 leaves because the field has structured into wages/employment, education-returns, immigration, and discrimination as distinct research clusters. Finance gets 5 leaves (asset pricing, corporate, banking, household, real estate). Behavioral & experimental gets 3 leaves matching lab, field, and theory subcommunities.

subfields.name: Game Theory & Mechanism Design
subfields.tagline: Game theory, mechanism design, contract theory, social choice.

subfields.name: Market Design & Matching
subfields.tagline: Market design, matching theory, auction theory applied.

subfields.name: Decision Theory
subfields.tagline: Decision theory under risk and ambiguity, social choice, axiomatic models.

subfields.tagline: General equilibrium models, welfare economics theory.

subfields.tagline: Monetary policy, central banking research, inflation analysis.

subfields.tagline: Fiscal policy, government debt, fiscal multipliers, debt sustainability.

subfields.tagline: Growth theory, productivity, long-run macroeconomics, structural transformation.

subfields.tagline: Business-cycle models, DSGE, HANK heterogeneous-agent macro.

uncovered_concerns: Economic history folds under structural change in LMICs + monetary economics + growth at L3.
Applied microeconomics & policy folds across welfare program evaluation, education returns, discrimination-inequality at L3.
International trade & international economics fold under growth in LMICs + macroeconomics at L3.
